textPlain: I really just want to know how it compares to ChatGPT and Claude at various tasks, but there aren’t any graphs for that.

I made some dynamic GGUFs for the 32B MoE model! Try:./llama.cpp/llama-cli -hf unsloth/granite-4.0-h-small-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XLAlso a support agent finetuning notebook with granite 4: https://colab.research.google.com/github/unslothai/notebooks...

IBM announcement post is more informative than venturebeatIBM Granite 4.0: hyper-efficient, high performance hybrid models for enterprisehttps://www.ibm.com/new/announcements/ibm-granite-4-0-hyper-...

Tried out the Ollama version and it's insanely fast with really good results for 1.9GB size. Supposed to have a 1M context window, would be interested where the speed goes then.No Mamba in the Ollama version though.
Every technical paper I've read that IBM publish at an ML conference has been P-hacked to hell. Stay away.

ISO 42001 certified.> ISO/IEC 42001 is an international standard that specifies requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an Artificial Intelligence Management System (AIMS) within organizations. It is designed for entities providing or utilizing AI-based products or services, ensuring responsible development and use of AI systems.https://www.iso.org/standard/42001If anyone has access to ISO standards, I'm really curious what the practical effects of that certification is. I.e. what things does Granite have that others don't, because they had to add/do it to fulfill the certification.The committee was formed in 2017, chaired by an AI expert: https://www.iso.org/committee/6794475.html
(I've only just starting running local LLMs so excuse the dumb question).Would Granite run with llama.cpp and use Mamba?
